extractions.problem: Mutation-targeted therapy in cystic fibrosis is not available for patients carrying ultra-rare CFTR variants like W57G/A234D; efficacy of CFTR modulators on this genotype is unknown.
extractions.method: In silico structural modeling using cryo-EM CFTR structures (5UAK, 6O1V) for stability prediction, combined with functional characterization via western blotting, forskolin-induced swelling (FIS) assay in colonoids, and Ussing chamber analysis in reprogrammed nasal epithelial cells.
extractions.finding: Both mutations destabilize CFTR but W57G is ~10 kcal/mol more destabilizing than A234D; residual CFTR function increases with VX661±VX445±VX770 treatment; in vivo VX770 reduced sweat chloride to non-CF levels and increased FEV1% by 6% after 27 weeks.
extractions.dataset: Patient-derived colonoids and nasal epithelial cells (W57G/A234D genotype), FRT and CFBE cell lines, F508del/F508del and non-CF controls
extractions.metric: Sweat chloride concentration, predicted FEV1%, forskolin-induced swelling, CFTR protein band C expression, ΔΔG (kcal/mol)
extractions.baseline: Untreated (DMSO) conditions, non-CF controls, F508del/F508del genotype
extractions.result: 6% absolute FEV1% increase after 27 weeks VX770, sweat chloride reduced to non-CF levels, W57G destabilization ~10 kcal/mol greater than A234D

extractions.problem: The biochemistry of multi-cell systems (hepatocyte-endothelial-adipocyte) involving homeostatic balance in glucose and lipid metabolism is too complex to understand without computational models.
extractions.method: Modular ODE-based model using Michaelis-Menten kinetics implemented in Simulink, merging three validated single-cell metabolic models with physiological interconnections under static and dynamic (flow) conditions.
extractions.finding: Good agreement between simulated and experimental metabolic profiles for monocultures and connected three-culture system; heuristic correction factors identified differential pathway activation under flow vs static conditions.
extractions.dataset: In vitro connected three-culture system: 250K hepatocytes, 80K endothelial cells, 300K adipocytes in MCmB 2.0 bioreactor
extractions.metric: Glucose, fatty acid, lactate, glycerol concentration profiles over time
extractions.result: Good agreement between simulated and experimental metabolic profiles in both static and dynamic conditions

extractions.problem: Reliable personalized prediction of NSCLC response to chemotherapy requires estimation of in vivo cell-killing efficacy, which cannot be directly measured or adequately reproduced by in vitro experiments.
extractions.method: Multiscale mechanistic model incorporating uncontrolled proliferation, population heterogeneity, hypoxia, and treatment resistance; sensitivity analysis via OFAT and Latin hypercube sampling/partial rank correlation coefficient (LHS/PRCC) approaches.
extractions.finding: Volume growth rate and growth fraction at diagnosis are key determinants for accurate cell kill rate estimation; the framework provides quantitative CKR estimates for cisplatin-based doublet regimens applicable to personalized predictions.
extractions.dataset: 13 NSCLC patients (9 SCC, 4 ADC, stage III-IV) treated with cisplatin + gemcitabine/vinorelbine/docetaxel
extractions.metric: Cell kill rate (CKR), tumor volume change from CT imaging, partial rank correlation coefficients
extractions.baseline: Pre-treatment tumor volume (CT at two time points), exponential growth model
extractions.result: CKR estimates provided for 13 patients; volume growth rate and growth fraction identified as highest-sensitivity parameters via PRCC

extractions.problem: Genome-scale metabolic reconstructions include all reactions implied by genome annotation, but many are inactive in specific contexts; methods are needed to create context-specific networks for accurate in silico modeling.
extractions.method: GIMME (Gene Inactivity Moderated by Metabolism and Expression) algorithm using quantitative gene expression data and required metabolic functionalities with linear optimization to produce context-specific reconstructions and quantitative inconsistency scores.
extractions.finding: GIMME produces context-specific metabolic networks consistent with experimental results for E. coli adaptive evolution, metabolic engineering strain design, and human skeletal muscle cells; inconsistency scores quantify data-objective agreement.
extractions.dataset: E. coli adaptive evolution strains, E. coli metabolic engineering strains, human skeletal muscle cells (GEO expression data)
extractions.metric: Normalized consistency score (NCS), inconsistency score (IS), permutation test p-value
extractions.baseline: Genome-scale metabolic reconstruction (full network), random permutations (10,000 randomizations)
extractions.result: Consistency scores statistically significant (p<0.05 permutation test); context-specific predictions match known experimental phenotypes

extractions.problem: T-cell epitope prediction algorithms used for SARS-CoV-2 vaccine design have inherent limitations that may cause vaccine candidates to miss critically important immune responses.
extractions.method: Perspective/review analysis of limitations in HLA class I-restricted epitope prediction algorithms, including biases toward 9-mer/10-mer peptides and incomplete HLA allele coverage.
extractions.finding: Approaches exclusively based on 9-mer/10-mer epitope prediction will miss important T-cell responses; unbiased overlapping peptide panels should complement prediction-based approaches to define correlates of protective immunity.
extractions.baseline: 9-mer/10-mer HLA class I epitope prediction algorithms

extractions.problem: Metastatic melanoma resists TRAIL-induced apoptosis via pro-inflammatory NF-κB and STAT3 pathways, limiting therapeutic options that selectively kill cancer cells.
extractions.method: Boolean network modeling of melanoma signaling pathways with stuck-at fault analysis to simulate gene mutations and drug interventions; experimental validation using A375 melanoma cell viability assays.
extractions.finding: Cryptotanshinone increases TRAIL sensitivity through dual action on DR5 upregulation and STAT3 inhibition; it induces apoptosis even without TRAIL by downregulating anti-apoptotic factors, validated experimentally in A375 cells.
extractions.dataset: A375 metastatic melanoma cell line, Boolean network of TRAIL/NF-κB/STAT3 signaling pathways
extractions.metric: Apoptosis ratio (sum pro-apoptotic / sum anti-apoptotic factors), cell viability
extractions.result: CT induces apoptosis even without TRAIL input; enhanced cell death in A375 cells experimentally validated

extractions.problem: Gemcitabine has limited cellular uptake; pharmacokinetic properties of novel gemcitabine-cell-penetrating peptide conjugates need evaluation to assess therapeutic viability.
extractions.method: PCA-derived z-scale from 12 physicochemical descriptors of 20 amino acids for QSAR CPP prediction models; GastroPlus pharmacokinetic simulation of plasma concentration profiles for conjugates.
extractions.finding: CPP prediction models achieved ~80% accuracy; Gem-Cys-pVEC conjugate showed best in vitro bioactivity; GastroPlus simulations confirmed conjugates reach therapeutic plasma levels with bioavailability comparable to parental gemcitabine.
extractions.dataset: CellPPD database (900 CPP, 1148 non-CPP), MKN-28 and HT-29 cell lines
extractions.metric: Sensitivity, specificity, accuracy, MCC, Q², AUC (pharmacokinetic)
extractions.result: 79% sensitivity, 80% specificity, 81% accuracy, 61% MCC, Q²=0.406; conjugate AUC comparable to GEMZAR

extractions.problem: SMYD3 lysine methyltransferase is overexpressed in breast carcinomas and drives uncontrolled proliferation; a fast, affordable hit-to-lead methodology for identifying SMYD3 inhibitors is needed.
extractions.method: In silico docking screening of 137,990 ZINC15 molecules using Schrödinger software against SMYD3 binding pocket (PDB: 5EX3), followed by in vitro methyltransferase assays and breast cancer cell line experiments.
extractions.finding: Inhibitor-4 reduces SMYD3-mediated H3 methylation by 70%, restores normal proliferation rates, arrests cell cycle, and induces apoptosis in breast cancer cells without affecting wildtype cells.
extractions.dataset: MCF7 and MDA-MB-231 breast cancer cell lines, MCF10A normal cells, ZINC15 library (137,990 molecules)
extractions.metric: SMYD3 methyltransferase activity (% reduction), cell proliferation rate, Glide docking score (kJ/mol)
extractions.baseline: BCI-121 (known SMYD3 inhibitor), untreated controls, wildtype MCF10A cells
extractions.result: 70% reduction in H3 methylation, binding energy -7.2 to -9.1 kJ/mol, SMYD3 overexpressed 1.8-fold (MCF7) and 2.6-fold (MDA-MB-231) vs normal

extractions.problem: Preclinical identification of drug-induced cardiotoxicity and its translation to human proarrhythmic risk remain major challenges; animal models have species-specific limitations.
extractions.method: In silico drug trials on a population of 530 human cardiac Purkinje cell models for 14 reference compounds at multiple concentrations and pacing frequencies; comparison with in vitro rabbit Purkinje fiber recordings and clinical TdP risk from CredibleMeds.
extractions.finding: In silico human Purkinje EAD-based predictions achieved 100% accuracy for TdP risk classification, outperforming rabbit experiments (79%); high consistency between in silico and in vitro results with discrepancies explained by species differences.
extractions.dataset: 14 reference compounds, 530 in silico human Purkinje models, rabbit Purkinje fibers (n=4-6 per compound), CredibleMeds clinical TdP reports
extractions.metric: TdP risk classification accuracy, APD90, APD50, dV/dtMax, EAD occurrence, APA
extractions.baseline: In vitro rabbit Purkinje fiber experiments (79% accuracy), APD-based prediction (86% accuracy)
extractions.result: 100% accuracy (in silico EAD-based) vs 79% (rabbit in vitro) vs 86% (APD-based, both platforms) for proarrhythmic risk prediction

extractions.problem: Stone cells negatively affect pear fruit quality through secondary cell wall thickening and lignification; the KNOX gene family regulating these processes has not been characterized in pear.
extractions.method: Genome-wide identification of KNOX family in Pyrus bretschneideri using BLASTp and domain analysis; phylogenetic and synteny analysis across four Rosaceae species; overexpression of PbKNOX1 in Arabidopsis with lignin content and cell wall thickness measurements.
extractions.finding: 18 PbKNOX genes identified across 10 chromosomes; PbKNOX1 expression inversely correlates with stone cell and lignin accumulation; PbKNOX1 overexpression decreased secondary cell wall thickness by ~19% and lignin content by ~13% with downregulation of lignin biosynthesis genes.
extractions.dataset: Pyrus bretschneideri (Dangshan Su pear) at 8 developmental stages, Arabidopsis thaliana Columbia-0, grape/mei/strawberry genomes
extractions.metric: Secondary cell wall thickness, lignin content (%), qRT-PCR gene expression levels
extractions.result: ~19% decrease in vessel cell wall thickness, ~13% reduction in lignin content in PbKNOX1-overexpressing Arabidopsis lines
